purpose: This repository contains end-to-end testing infrastructure for Cross River Bank's lending and selling platform, simulating the entire selling environment with mock services and automated tests to validate loan origination, processing, interest accrual, and purchasing workflows.
businessFeatures: Loan origination and purchase flow testing
Contract management and volume fee processing
Loan type grooming and change approval workflows

apis.purpose: Get accounts for loan actions based on MPL, bank and loan type
Get accounts for changing loan type
Enable or disable transfer delays in the COS mock service

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity with sale status, origination data, and financial details

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Configuration for interest calculation, timing, and fee structures

dataEntities.name: AccountConfig
dataEntities.description: Bank account configuration for different transaction types

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Group of loans processed together for purchase

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Actions performed on loans like purchase, interest accrual

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Payment Queue
messagingPatterns.description: Queue for processing payment completion events

messagingPatterns.name: LoanSaleStatusChanged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when loan sale status changes

messagingPatterns.name: BatchCompleted
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when batch purchase completes

messagingPatterns.name: TrueUpCharged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when true-up volume fee is charged

messagingPatterns.name: InvestorChanged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when loan investor changes

messagingPatterns.name: LoanTypeChanged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when loan type is changed

findings.title: Hard-coded secrets and credentials
findings.description: Several files contain hard-coded credentials or references to injecting AWS credentials directly in scripts. This is a security risk. Implement a secure secret management solution or use environment variables consistently.

findings.title: Insufficient error handling in payment processing
findings.description: The COS simulator does not properly handle transaction failures and always returns success responses. This could mask real payment processing issues in tests. Add error simulation capabilities and verify error handling paths.
